tests.title: Successful token revocation returns HTTP 200 regardless of token validity
tests.given: Authenticated client credentials for the revocation endpoint and either a valid token or an already-expired/revoked/unknown token
tests.when: Revoke is called with the token parameter
tests.then: A POST application/x-www-form-urlencoded request carrying token is sent and any HTTP 200 response (empty body or empty JSON object) yields a success result; the response MUST NOT differ between valid and invalid/expired/unknown tokens, preventing token scanning

tests.title: token_type_hint is included in the request
tests.given: A token and a token_type_hint of access_token or refresh_token
tests.when: Revoke is called with the hint configured
tests.then: The hint is sent as the token_type_hint form parameter; the token parameter is always present and the request succeeds regardless of hint correctness (the server MAY use the hint to optimize lookup but MUST accept the request even if it is incorrect)

tests.title: unsupported_token_type error is surfaced
tests.given: A revocation request for a token type the server does not support revoking
tests.when: Revoke is called and the endpoint replies HTTP 400 with an OAuth error body
tests.then: A typed revocation error carrying error=unsupported_token_type (plus optional error_description, error_uri, and the HTTP status) is surfaced

tests.title: invalid_client error is surfaced
tests.when: Revoke is called and the endpoint replies HTTP 401 with an OAuth error body
tests.then: A typed revocation error carrying error=invalid_client (plus optional error_description, error_uri, and the HTTP status) is surfaced

tests.title: Discovery-based endpoint resolution
tests.given: A valid OIDC Discovery / Authorization Server Metadata document containing revocation_endpoint
tests.when: The revocation endpoint is resolved from discovery and passed to Revoke
tests.then: The revocation_endpoint URL from the discovery document is used rather than requiring manual configuration
